questions.q: What was the year in which the modern Olympic Games were revived?
What is the motto of the Olympic Games?
Which of the following is NOT one of the Fundamental Principles of the Olympic Charter?
Explain the significance of the Olympic Oath and the Olympic Flag.
questions.answer: The Olympic Oath is taken by the athletes to pledge their commitment to abide by the rules of the game, while the Olympic Flag symbolizes the unity of nations and promotes international friendship.

questions.q: Who is the founder of the modern Olympic Games?
State the meaning of the Olympic motto.
questions.answer: The Olympic motto 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' means 'Faster, Higher, Stronger'.

questions.q: Which of the following is a fundamental principle of the Olympic Charter?
Explain the role of the International Olympic Committee (IOC) in the Olympic Movement.
questions.answer: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is responsible for promoting the Olympic Movement, selecting the host city for the Olympic Games, and ensuring the integrity and fairness of the Games.

questions.q: What is the significance of the Olympic Torch Relay?
questions.answer: The Olympic Torch Relay symbolizes the spirit of solidarity and friendship among nations.

questions.q: Who is responsible for the establishment of the Olympic Movement?
questions.answer: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) is responsible for the establishment and development of the Olympic Movement.
